company_type: Beverage Manufacturing - Beer and Adjacent Products

dependencies: Small number of suppliers for aluminum cans and bottles, glass bottles, paperboard and carbon dioxide
Consolidation of packaging materials suppliers has reduced local supply alternatives
Hops purchased from suppliers in U.S. and Europe primarily sourced from Germany, U.K., Czech Republic and Slovenia
Barley purchased primarily from western U.S. and Canadian Prairies
Majority of brewing materials in EMEA&APAC provided by suppliers based in Europe
Reliance on third-party logistics providers for distribution
Dependence on independent distributors in U.S. with exclusive territories protected by state statutes
risks: Inability of suppliers to meet production requirements without sufficient time to develop alternative source
Consolidation of packaging materials suppliers reduced local supply alternatives and increased risks of supply disruptions
Demand for input materials has increased resulting in shortage of capacity and increases in costs
Financial deterioration of suppliers could adversely impact business
Supply chain constraints and disruptions caused by Russia-Ukraine conflict and uncertain economic environment
Inflationary pressures on commodity and other input prices
Concentration risk with small number of suppliers for key packaging materials

regulatory_risks: Changes in tax, environmental, trade or other regulations
Failure to comply with existing licensing, trade and other regulations
Excise tax increases in jurisdictions where products are sold
Changes to U.S. or foreign trade policies, impositions of new or increased tariffs
U.S. aluminum tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico and EU
Proposed 25% tariff on all imports from Canada announced in February 2025
European Union-United Kingdom Trade and Cooperating Agreement disruptions
Changes to interprovincial trade rules, regulations, distribution models in Canada
Ontario beer market expansion to convenience stores and grocery stores
Product labeling or warning requirements or limitations on availability of beverages
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) programs for packaging
Deposit return schemes (DRS) for drinks containers
Taxes on packaging material
Environmental regulations to discourage use of particular materials
OECD global minimum tax framework (15% minimum tax effective 2023)
EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive
SEC cybersecurity rules
Data privacy laws including GDPR, California Privacy Rights Act, Virginia Consumer Data Protection Act, Colorado Privacy Act
Regulation limiting or banning manufacturing, distribution or sale of alcoholic beverages
Health warning labels on alcohol beverage products (Ireland law effective May 2026, U.S. Surgeon General advisory January 2025)
Europe Beating Cancer Plan proposals for mandatory health warnings
Minimum unit pricing for alcohol
Advertising and marketing restrictions on alcohol
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, U.K. Bribery Act, U.K. Proceeds of Crime Act compliance

sanction_risks: Russia-Ukraine conflict resulted in suspension of exports to Russia and termination of license to produce brands in Russia
Heightened economic sanctions from international community including U.S., U.K. and European Union due to Russia-Ukraine conflict
Potential for broader impacts expanding into other markets from Russia-Ukraine conflict
Increased trade barriers or restrictions on global trade
U.S. aluminum tariffs creating volatility and increased costs
Proposed U.S. tariffs on Canadian imports (25% announced February 2025, subsequently suspended)
Export control, sanctions and investment restrictions
